verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a predicted soluble protein with no transmembrane domain (confirmed in literature). It localizes to the intra-acrosomal compartment of sperm and is lost after the acrosome reaction, consistent with it being an intra-acrosomal luminal protein rather than a surface-exposed one. The NCBI summary places it in the nucleus. Protein Atlas notes cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells. Unlike IZUMO1 (a TM protein that translocates to the sperm surface after the acrosome reaction), IZUMO4 lacks a TM domain, has no documented outer-leaflet display, no therapeutic surface-targeting programs, and no cell-state-induced ecto-form in the literature. Germline/sperm-restricted expression is the only context, and within that context it remains intra-acrosomal/soluble.
